edge_hypothesis: The MACD tail harvester 8:1 on ETH/1h was the FIRST system ACCEPT ever (PF=1.442, DD=7.1%, 156 trades) — yet it remains an isolated data point. Every subsequent MACD R:R experiment was on 4h (3.5:1→1.347, 5:1→1.353, 6:1→tested, 7:1→1.712, 9:1→tested, 12:1→1.302). The 1h timeframe may have a FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT R:R curve than 4h because: (1) 1h bars have 4x more signals per day, allowing more re-entries after tight-stop exits — this means the denominator (total trades) is higher, which can stabilize PF; (2) 1h ATR is proportionally smaller than 4h ATR, so absolute stop distances are tighter and TP targets are smaller — this changes the probability of hitting extreme TP levels; (3) 1h captures intra-day momentum that 4h smooths over — MACD histogram zero-crosses on 1h reflect 4-hour momentum shifts, not 16-hour trend changes. This spec maps three untested points on the 1h R:R curve: 5:1, 6:1, and 10:1. Combined with the existing 8:1→PF=1.442, this gives 4 data points to identify the 1h peak. If 1h peaks at a DIFFERENT R:R than 4h, the optimal forward-test should use timeframe-specific R:R ratios — not a single universal ratio. Regime assumption: 1h MACD champion showed ranging PF=1.795 >> trending PF=1.505 — ranging-dominant like 4h. Invalidation: (1) if the 1h curve shape exactly mirrors 4h (peak at 7-8:1, decline at 10:1+), then R:R optimization is timeframe-invariant and a single ratio works; (2) if 1h at 5:1 produces PF > 1.4 (higher than 4h's 1.353 at 5:1), then 1h is UNIFORMLY better than 4h at all R:R points — not just at 8:1 — and 1h should be the primary timeframe.

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with 5:1 R:R tail harvester (1.0x ATR stop / 5.0x ATR TP). Direct comparison to the 4h 5:1 ACCEPT (PF=1.353). On 4h, 5:1 is the moderate tail harvester entry point. On 1h, the faster signal cadence may produce more trades at this R:R — if PF holds, 1h at 5:1 could be the highest-trade-count ACCEPT in the system. Expected: 250+ trades (1h generates 4x more MACD zero-crosses than 4h), PF depending on whether 1h signal quality matches 4h at this R:R.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: 1h at 5:1 should produce more trades than 4h at 5:1 with comparable PF — highest-volume tail harvester candidate
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: Close > EMA_50 trend bias — same as all MACD variants
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — proven highest-quality entry signal on ETH/1h
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 5.0x TP — moderate tail harvester, matching 4h ACCEPT R:R for cross-timeframe comparison

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_6to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with 6:1 R:R (1.0x ATR stop / 6.0x ATR TP). Interpolation point between 5:1 and the 8:1 champion. This R:R was recently tested on 4h — comparing the 1h result to the 4h result at the same R:R isolates the timeframe variable. If 1h at 6:1 > 4h at 6:1, faster signal cadence improves edge at this R:R. If 1h at 6:1 < 4h at 6:1, the larger 4h bar structure produces higher-quality signals.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: 1h at 6:1 vs 4h at 6:1 isolates the timeframe variable — determines if faster cadence helps or hurts at this R:R
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: Close > EMA_50 trend bias — standard MACD baseline
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — highest-quality entry on 1h, testing cross-timeframe R:R comparison
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 6.0x TP — 6:1 interpolation, 1h-vs-4h comparison at same R:R

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_10to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with EXTREME R:R (10:1) using 1.0x ATR stop / 10.0x ATR TP. The 4h curve showed decline at 12:1 (PF=1.302 vs 8:1 peak PF=1.442). But 1h bars are smaller — a 10x ATR move on 1h covers less absolute price distance than on 4h, making it potentially more achievable. The hypothesis: 1h R:R curve may peak LATER than 4h because smaller ATR values mean extreme multiples are still reachable within intra-day trends. If 1h at 10:1 produces PF > 1.3, the 1h curve has NOT peaked at 8:1 and extends further. This would mean the optimal 1h strategy uses higher R:R than the optimal 4h strategy — a timeframe-specific optimization insight. Expected: ~156 trades (same signal cadence), ~12% win rate.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (MACD histogram zero-cross bearish).

Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Hypothesis: 1h ATR is smaller, so 10x ATR is more achievable on 1h than 4h — curve may peak later on 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.0x ATR_14 from entry.
